updates.tweet_text:
Bears control under 7509 now in #ES_F. Most recent long trigger was the 7427 Failed Breakdown given explicitly in newsletter. 7427 was also 1st support today given at 8am here. Just tested exact, but its very weak now

7452, 7462, 7472, 7509 if bulls have energy. 7409, 7398 below https://t.co/vBJ3xxIUtf

updates.tweet_text:
7427 Failed Breakdown continue to pay out heavily in #ES_F and as repeated non-stop, volatility makes our job very easy. We just trapped 7427 again down to 7416, recovered, pushed.

Targets given were 7452 (hit), 7462 (hit), 7472, 7509 next. Just ride your runner now https://t.co/kyfPMqK075
updates.notes: All prices mentioned (7427, 7452, 7462, 7472, 7509) are morning-plan levels or observations of hits; 7416 is a passing reference, not named as actionable.

updates.tweet_text: @joeo1s Yes, when volatility spikes like this we tend to get those big failed breakdowns. Its why even though I am a long only trader, I make far more in red markets than green markets. If it were my choice we'd always be in a bear market!
updates.notes: Pure conversational commentary about volatility and trading style; no price levels named or implied.

updates.tweet_text: @JBonwax Great to hear, and thanks for the subscribe!
updates.notes: Tweet is a social reply with no price levels, no actionable language, and no market content whatsoever.

updates.tweet_text:
We are now into standard mid-day chop in #ES_F and as always, chop s not to be traded. We trade deep flushes and Failed Breakdowns. The 7427 Failed Breakdown after 930AM was last long trigger. Targets were 7452 (hit), 7462 (hit), 7472 (hit).

7509, then 7523 are next. Magnets https://t.co/gxDzywAh3Q
updates.notes: All prices mentioned (7427, 7452, 7462, 7472, 7509, 7523) are either morning-plan levels or reaffirmations/hit targets. No new actionable levels introduced.

updates.tweet_text: @RoyMcAvoyGolf THat is literally the point of the Failed Breakdown. It stops everyone out, then you get in afterwwards
updates.notes: Reply explaining FBD concept to a user; no price levels named, purely educational/conversational content.

updates.tweet_text: @chrisAn00930062 I don't buy any levels, I trade Failed Breakdowns. I don't buy supports ever. When I say a level is weak, it means the level is likely to fail (which it did). Then we can long the Failed Breakdown. That's my whole system
updates.notes: Tweet is a methodology explanation to a user, no actionable price levels named whatsoever.

updates.tweet_text: Its pure mid-day chop now in #ES_F, no trading chop. Trade only big volatility expansions, otherwise, just ride runner. 7427 Failed Breakdown after 930am was the last long. 7452, 7462, 7472 (all hit) were targets. Now, hold runner for the next magnet which is 7509/7523 https://t.co/4mH1wAaHkQ
updates.notes: All prices (7427, 7452, 7462, 7472, 7509, 7523) are either morning-plan levels, already-hit targets, or trade management/runner instructions — no new actionable levels introduced.

updates.tweet_text: Closing update #ES_F: Newsletter out soon talking next entries from here. Bulls ideally want to hold 7424 overnight now (or quick trap below). The magnet is now 7509, then 7530. 7530 must  backtest

updates.tweet_text:
This market remains all about one level ~7425. It was the Failed Breakdown here after 9:30AM yesterday that got us long for the rally to 7472 target. Last eve #ES_F tested 7425 *again* and bounced.

Plan today: Just ride your runner. 7463, 7472, 7492, 7510=targets. 7425=support https://t.co/G52V8jfV5W

updates.tweet_text: Note: There is nothing to do here but ride runner - no change to newsletter entries. Readers know I do not trade low volatility chop. For me to even open my platform I need a volatility expansion/elevator down sell. If you engage chop, you are trading noise.
updates.notes: Pure trade management/commentary tweet. No prices named at all. Describes a no-trade condition (low volatility chop) with no actionable levels.

updates.tweet_text: @mainftr Yes quite often...usually volatility also dies mid day. Not always though. Most days we get at least one good volatility expansion typically in then morning. Then I make my trade and shut down the platform. Can't trade low volatility
updates.notes: Pure conversational reply about trading habits and volatility patterns. No prices mentioned, no actionable levels named.
